description: Tessellate nodes. The nodes are tessellated in the current geometry set using the Qhull ( http://www.qhull.org ) package. Polygons are created in the current set, by default. See the to-set and start-set keywords to output polygons to different geometry sets. If the set keyword is not given, then the current set is used.

keywords.name: box-check
keywords.description: Only create Voronoi cells where the cell node positions fall within the axis-aligned bounding box enclosing all seed geometry nodes.

keywords.name: convex-hull
keywords.description: Calculate the convex hull of the nodes in the current geometry set. This keyword is not compatible with the start-set .

keywords.name: delaunay
keywords.description: Calculate the Delaunay simplices of the nodes of the current geometry set.

keywords.name: start-set
keywords.description: Each Delaunay simplex or Voronoi region is placed in a unique set. Each set name is a combination of s with the ID of the set post-pended to s . For example, the set with ID = 13 with s = con would have name con13 . This keyword cannot be used with the convexhull keyword.

keywords.name: to-set
keywords.description: Place the resulting polygons in the set with name s . This set is created if it does not exist.

keywords.name: voronoi
keywords.description: Calculate the Voronoi regions of the nodes in the current geometry set. Note Voronoi regions with points at infinity are skipped when producing the output.

keywords.name: convex-hull
keywords.description: Calculate the convex hull of the nodes in the current geometry set. This keyword is not compatible with the start-set .

keywords.name: delaunay
keywords.description: Calculate the Delaunay simplices of the nodes of the current geometry set.

keywords.name: start-set
